meta_title: For Students: email automation to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to email automation and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Email Automation to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with no project ideas. Learn to build an automated email system using Antigravity and Claude Code in this intermediate student checklist.
content.answer_block: Overcoming the 'no project ideas' paralysis requires building utility-focused tools. Email automation is the perfect intermediate project: it teaches API integration, cron-job scheduling, and data parsing. By using Antigravity to scaffold your infrastructure and Claude Code for iterative development, you transform a blank screen into a functional, portfolio-ready product.
content.intro:
The blinking cursor on an empty IDE is the graveyard of potential careers. You've mastered the syntax, you've watched the tutorials, but now you're paralyzed by the 'what do I build?' syndrome. This isn't a failure of skill; it's a failure of scope. You need a project that isn't just a toy, but a tool that solves a real-world friction point.

Email automation is your gateway to professional-grade development. It forces you to handle authentication, rate limiting, and asynchronous workflows—the exact skills hiring managers hunt for. We are going to bypass the boilerplate and build a robust, automated notification engine that turns your coding frustration into a tangible, high-value asset for your portfolio.

steps.title: Define the Automation Logic
steps.description: Before writing code, map out the trigger-action flow. We'll focus on a 'Price Watcher' alert system to practice API data fetching.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a project architecture for a price-tracking email service. Define the data schema for tracking product URLs and the trigger logic for sending alerts via SendGrid API.

steps.title: Configure the Environment with Antigravity
steps.description: Use Antigravity to scaffold the environment, ensuring all dependencies and environment variables are strictly typed and secured.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a secure configuration module using Pydantic for environment variables, including keys for SendGrid and the target database.

steps.title: Implement the Data Scraper
steps.description: Build a resilient scraper that extracts product pricing. Use Claude Code to handle DOM parsing errors gracefully.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust scraper function using BeautifulSoup that handles 403 errors and extracts price text from a generic product page structure.

steps.title: Integrate Email Dispatcher
steps.description: Connect to an SMTP relay or API to send the actual emails. This is where your system gains its 'utility' status.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a service module that composes HTML email templates and sends them using the SendGrid SDK, including a retry mechanism for failed deliveries.

steps.title: Schedule and Deploy
steps.description: Finalize the automation loop so it runs autonomously, proving your capability to deploy production-ready code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a GitHub Action workflow file that runs the email automation script every 24 hours.
steps.claude_code_command: claude commit --message='Setup automated daily execution pipeline'

pro_tips: Always implement exponential backoff in your API calls to avoid getting blacklisted by scrapers.
Use environment-specific secrets files; never hardcode credentials, even in a private repo.
Build a 'dry-run' mode into your CLI tool to test email logic without actually spamming your inbox.
Log every failed attempt to a local SQLite file so you can debug 'silent' failures later.
Add a 'Unsubscribe' header to your automated emails; it’s a standard practice that shows you care about deliverability and compliance.
content.next_steps: Once your script is running, add a basic UI using Streamlit to allow users to input their own URLs, turning this from a script into a full-featured SaaS tool.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a student following tutorials; you are an engineer shipping production-grade automation. This project is the first step toward a portfolio that demands attention. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
